krv.COL_1_1: 하나님의 뜻으로 말미암아 그리스도 예수의 사도 된 바울과 형제 디모데는
krv.COL_1_2: 골로새에 있는 성도들 곧 그리스도 안에서 신실한 형제들에게 편지하노니 우리 아버지 하나님으로부터 은혜와 평강이 너희에게 있을지어다
krv.COL_1_3: 우리가 너희를 위하여 기도할 때마다 하나님 곧 우리 주 예수 그리스도의 아버지께 감사하노라
krv.COL_1_4: 이는 그리스도 예수 안에 너희의 믿음과 모든 성도에 대한 사랑을 들음이요
krv.COL_1_5: 너희를 위하여 하늘에 쌓아 둔 소망을 인함이니 곧 너희가 전에 복음 진리의 말씀을 들은 것이라
krv.COL_1_6: 이 복음이 이미 너희에게 이르매 너희가 듣고 참으로 하나님의 은혜를 깨달은 날부터 너희 중에서와 같이 또한 온 천하에서도 열매를 맺어 자라는도다
krv.COL_1_7: 이와 같이 우리와 함께 종 된 사랑하는 에바브라에게 너희가 배웠나니 그는 너희를 위하여 그리스도의 신실한 일꾼이요
krv.COL_1_8: 성령 안에서 너희 사랑을 우리에게 고한 자니라
krv.COL_1_9: 이로써 우리도 듣던 날부터 너희를 위하여 기도하기를 그치지 아니하고 구하노니 너희로 하여금 모든 신령한 지혜와 총명에 하나님의 뜻을 아는 것으로 채우게 하시고
krv.COL_1_10: 주께 합당히 행하여 범사에 기쁘시게 하고 모든 선한 일에 열매를 맺게 하시며 하나님을 아는 것에 자라게 하시고
krv.COL_1_11: 그 영광의 힘을 좇아 모든 능력으로 능하게 하시며 기쁨으로 모든 견딤과 오래 참음에 이르게 하시고
krv.COL_1_12: 우리로 하여금 빛 가운데서 성도의 기업의 부분을 얻기에 합당하게 하신 아버지께 감사하게 하시기를 원하노라
krv.COL_1_13: 그가 우리를 흑암의 권세에서 건져내사 그의 사랑의 아들의 나라로 옮기셨으니
krv.COL_1_14: 그 아들 안에서 우리가 구속 곧 죄 사함을 얻었도다
krv.COL_1_15: 그는 보이지 아니하시는 하나님의 형상이요 모든 창조물보다 먼저 나신 자니
krv.COL_1_16: 만물이 그에게 창조되되 하늘과 땅에서 보이는 것들과 보이지 않는 것들과 혹은 보좌들이나 주관들이나 정사들이나 권세들이나 만물이 다 그로 말미암고 그를 위하여 창조되었고
krv.COL_1_17: 또한 그가 만물보다 먼저 계시고 만물이 그 안에 함께 섰느니라
krv.COL_1_18: 그는 몸인 교회의 머리라 그가 근본이요 죽은 자들 가운데서 먼저 나신 자니 이는 친히 만물의 으뜸이 되려 하심이요
krv.COL_1_19: 아버지께서는 모든 충만으로 예수 안에 거하게 하시고
krv.COL_1_20: 그의 십자가의 피로 화평을 이루사 만물 곧 땅에 있는 것들이나 하늘에 있는 것들을 그로 말미암아 자기와 화목케 되기를 기뻐하심이라
krv.COL_1_21: 전에 악한 행실로 멀리 떠나 마음으로 원수가 되었던 너희를
krv.COL_1_22: 이제는 그의 육체의 죽음으로 말미암아 화목케 하사 너희를 거룩하고 흠 없고 책망할 것이 없는 자로 그 앞에 세우고자 하셨으니
krv.COL_1_23: 만일 너희가 믿음에 거하고 터 위에 굳게 서서 너희 들은 바 복음의 소망에서 흔들리지 아니하면 그리하리라 이 복음은 천하 만민에게 전파된 바요 나 바울은 이 복음의 일꾼이 되었노라
krv.COL_1_24: 내가 이제 너희를 위하여 받는 괴로움을 기뻐하고 그리스도의 남은 고난을 그의 몸된 교회를 위하여 내 육체에 채우노라
krv.COL_1_25: 내가 교회 일꾼 된 것은 하나님이 너희를 위하여 내게 주신 경륜을 따라 하나님의 말씀을 이루려 함이니라
krv.COL_1_26: 이 비밀은 만세와 만대로부터 옴으로 감취었던 것인데 이제는 그의 성도들에게 나타났고
krv.COL_1_27: 하나님이 그들로 하여금 이 비밀의 영광이 이방인 가운데 어떻게 풍성한 것을 알게 하려 하심이라 이 비밀은 너희 안에 계신 그리스도시니 곧 영광의 소망이니라
krv.COL_1_28: 우리가 그를 전파하여 각 사람을 권하고 모든 지혜로 각 사람을 가르침은 각 사람을 그리스도 안에서 완전한 자로 세우려 함이니
krv.COL_1_29: 이를 위하여 나도 내 속에서 능력으로 역사하시는 이의 역사를 따라 힘을 다하여 수고하노라
krv.COL_2_1: 내가 너희와 라오디게아에 있는 자들과 무릇 내 육신의 얼굴을 보지 못한 자들을 위하여 어떻게 힘쓰는 것을 너희가 알기를 원하노니
krv.COL_2_2: 이는 저희로 마음에 위안을 받고 사랑 안에서 연합하여 원만한 이해의 모든 부요에 이르러 하나님의 비밀인 그리스도를 깨닫게 하려 함이라
krv.COL_2_3: 그 안에는 지혜와 지식의 모든 보화가 감취어 있느니라
krv.COL_2_4: 내가 이것을 말함은 아무도 공교한 말로 너희를 속이지 못하게 하려 함이니
krv.COL_2_5: 이는 내가 육신으로는 떠나 있으나 심령으로는 너희와 함께 있어 너희의 규모와 그리스도를 믿는 너희 믿음의 굳은 것을 기쁘게 봄이라
krv.COL_2_6: 그러므로 너희가 그리스도 예수를 주로 받았으니 그 안에서 행하되
krv.COL_2_7: 그 안에 뿌리를 박으며 세움을 입어 교훈을 받은 대로 믿음에 굳게 서서 감사함을 넘치게 하라
krv.COL_2_8: 누가 철학과 헛된 속임수로 너희를 노략할까 주의하라 이것이 사람의 유전과 세상의 초등학문을 좇음이요 그리스도를 좇음이 아니니라
krv.COL_2_9: 그 안에는 신성의 모든 충만이 육체로 거하시고
krv.COL_2_10: 너희도 그 안에서 충만하여졌으니 그는 모든 정사와 권세의 머리시라
krv.COL_2_11: 또 그 안에서 너희가 손으로 하지 아니한 할례를 받았으니 곧 육적 몸을 벗는 것이요 그리스도의 할례니라
krv.COL_2_12: 너희가 세례로 그리스도와 함께 장사한 바 되고 또 죽은 자들 가운데서 그를 일으키신 하나님의 역사를 믿음으로 말미암아 그 안에서 함께 일으키심을 받았느니라
krv.COL_2_13: 또 너희의 범죄와 육체의 무할례로 죽었던 너희를 하나님이 그와 함께 살리시고 우리에게 모든 죄를 사하시고
krv.COL_2_14: 우리를 거스리고 우리를 대적하는 의문에 쓴 증서를 도말하시고 제하여 버리사 십자가에 못 박으시고
krv.COL_2_15: 정사와 권세를 벗어버려 밝히 드러내시고 십자가로 승리하셨느니라
krv.COL_2_16: 그러므로 먹고 마시는 것과 절기나 월삭이나 안식일을 인하여 누구든지 너희를 폄론하지 못하게 하라
krv.COL_2_17: 이것들은 장래 일의 그림자이나 몸은 그리스도의 것이니라
krv.COL_2_18: 누구든지 일부러 겸손함과 천사 숭배함을 인하여 너희 상을 빼앗지 못하게 하라 저가 그 본 것을 의지하여 그 육체의 마음을 좇아 헛되이 과장하고
krv.COL_2_19: 머리를 붙들지 아니하는지라 온 몸이 머리로 말미암아 마디와 힘줄로 공급함을 얻고 연합하여 하나님이 자라게 하심으로 자라느니라
krv.COL_2_20: 너희가 세상의 초등 학문에서 그리스도와 함께 죽었거든 어찌하여 세상에 사는 것과 같이 의문에 순종하느냐
krv.COL_2_21: 곧 붙잡지도 말고 맛보지도 말고 만지지도 말라 하는 것이니
krv.COL_2_22: (이 모든 것은 쓰는 대로 부패에 돌아가리라) 사람의 명과 가르침을 좇느냐
krv.COL_2_23: 이런 것들은 자의적 숭배와 겸손과 몸을 괴롭게 하는 데 지혜 있는 모양이나 오직 육체 좇는 것을 금하는 데는 유익이 조금도 없느니라
krv.COL_3_1: 그러므로 너희가 그리스도와 함께 다시 살리심을 받았으면 위엣 것을 찾으라 거기는 그리스도께서 하나님 우편에 앉아 계시느니라
krv.COL_3_2: 위엣 것을 생각하고 땅엣 것을 생각지 말라
krv.COL_3_3: 이는 너희가 죽었고 너희 생명이 그리스도와 함께 하나님 안에 감취었음이니라
krv.COL_3_4: 우리 생명이신 그리스도께서 나타나실 그 때에 너희도 그와 함께 영광 중에 나타나리라
krv.COL_3_5: 그러므로 땅에 있는 지체를 죽이라 곧 음란과 부정과 사욕과 악한 정욕과 탐심이니 탐심은 우상 숭배니라
krv.COL_3_7: 너희도 전에 그 가운데 살 때에는 그 가운데서 행하였으나
krv.COL_3_8: 이제는 너희가 이 모든 것을 벗어버리라 곧 분과 악의와 훼방과 너희 입의 부끄러운 말이라
krv.COL_3_9: 너희가 서로 거짓말을 말라 옛 사람과 그 행위를 벗어버리고
krv.COL_3_10: 새 사람을 입었으니 이는 자기를 창조하신 자의 형상을 좇아 지식에까지 새롭게 하심을 받는 자니라
krv.COL_3_11: 거기는 헬라인과 유대인이나 할례당과 무할례당이나 야인이나 스구디아인이나 종이나 자유인이 분별이 있을 수 없나니 오직 그리스도는 만유시요 만유 안에 계시니라
krv.COL_3_12: 그러므로 너희는 하나님의 택하신 거룩하고 사랑하신 자처럼 긍휼과 자비와 겸손과 온유와 오래 참음을 옷입고
krv.COL_3_13: 누가 뉘게 혐의가 있거든 서로 용납하여 피차 용서하되 주께서 너희를 용서하신 것과 같이 너희도 그리하고
krv.COL_3_14: 이 모든 것 위에 사랑을 더하라 이는 온전하게 매는 띠니라
krv.COL_3_15: 그리스도의 평강이 너희 마음을 주장하게 하라 평강을 위하여 너희가 한 몸으로 부르심을 받았나니 또한 너희는 감사하는 자가 되라
krv.COL_3_16: 그리스도의 말씀이 너희 속에 풍성히 거하여 모든 지혜로 피차 가르치며 권면하고 시와 찬미와 신령한 노래를 부르며 마음에 감사함으로 하나님을 찬양하고
krv.COL_3_17: 또 무엇을 하든지 말에나 일에나 다 주 예수의 이름으로 하고 그를 힘입어 하나님 아버지께 감사하라
krv.COL_3_18: 아내들아 남편에게 복종하라 이는 주 안에서 마땅하니라
krv.COL_3_19: 남편들아 아내를 사랑하며 괴롭게 하지 말라
krv.COL_3_20: 자녀들아 모든 일에 부모에게 순종하라 이는 주 안에서 기쁘게 하는 것이니라
krv.COL_3_21: 아비들아 너희 자녀를 격노케 말지니 낙심할까 함이라
krv.COL_3_22: 종들아 모든 일에 육신의 상전들에게 순종하되 사람을 기쁘게 하는 자와 같이 눈가림만 하지 말고 오직 주를 두려워하여 성실한 마음으로 하라
krv.COL_3_23: 무슨 일을 하든지 마음을 다하여 주께 하듯 하고 사람에게 하듯 하지 말라
krv.COL_3_24: 이는 유업의 상을 주께 받을 줄 앎이니 너희는 주 그리스도를 섬기느니라
krv.COL_3_25: 불의를 행하는 자는 불의의 보응을 받으리니 주는 외모로 사람을 취하심이 없느니라
krv.COL_4_1: 상전들아 의와 공평을 종들에게 베풀지니 너희에게도 하늘에 상전이 계심을 알지어다
krv.COL_4_2: 기도를 항상 힘쓰고 기도에 감사함으로 깨어 있으라
krv.COL_4_3: 또한 우리를 위하여 기도하되 하나님이 전도할 문을 우리에게 열어주사 그리스도의 비밀을 말하게 하시기를 구하라 내가 이것을 인하여 매임을 당하였노라
krv.COL_4_4: 그리하면 내가 마땅히 할 말로써 이 비밀을 나타내리라
krv.COL_4_5: 외인을 향하여서는 지혜로 행하여 세월을 아끼라
krv.COL_4_6: 너희 말을 항상 은혜 가운데서 소금으로 고루게 함 같이 하라 그리하면 각 사람에게 마땅히 대답할 것을 알리라
krv.COL_4_7: 두기고가 내 사정을 다 너희에게 알게 하리니 그는 사랑을 받는 형제요 신실한 일꾼이요 주 안에서 함께 된 종이라
krv.COL_4_8: 내가 저를 특별히 너희에게 보낸 것은 너희로 우리 사정을 알게 하고 너희 마음을 위로하게 하려 함이라
krv.COL_4_9: 신실하고 사랑을 받는 형제 오네시모를 함께 보내노니 그는 너희에게서 온 사람이라 저희가 여기 일을 다 너희에게 알게 하리라
krv.COL_4_10: 나와 함께 갇힌 아리스다고와 바나바의 생질 마가와 (이 마가에 대하여 너희가 명을 받았으매 그가 이르거든 영접하라)
krv.COL_4_11: 유스도라 하는 예수도 너희에게 문안하니 저희는 할례당이라 이들만 하나님 나라를 위하여 함께 역사하는 자들이니 이런 사람들이 나의 위로가 되었느니라
krv.COL_4_12: 그리스도 예수의 종인 너희에게서 온 에바브라가 너희에게 문안하니 저가 항상 너희를 위하여 애써 기도하여 너희로 하나님의 모든 뜻 가운데서 완전하고 확신 있게 서기를 구하나니
krv.COL_4_13: 그가 너희와 라오디게아에 있는 자들과 히에라볼리에 있는 자들을 위하여 많이 수고하는 것을 내가 증거하노라
krv.COL_4_14: 사랑을 받는 의원 누가와 또 데마가 너희에게 문안하느니라
krv.COL_4_15: 라오디게아에 있는 형제들과 눔바와 그 여자의 집에 있는 교회에 문안하고
krv.COL_4_16: 이 편지를 너희에게서 읽은 후에 라오디게아인의 교회에서도 읽게 하고 또 라오디게아로서 오는 편지를 너희도 읽으라
krv.COL_4_17: 아킵보에게 이르기를 주 안에서 받은 직분을 삼가 이루라고 하라
krv.COL_4_18: 나 바울은 친필로 문안하노니 나의 매인 것을 생각하라 은혜가 너희에게 있을지어다

kjv.COL_1_1: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ by the will of God, and Timotheus our brother,
kjv.COL_1_2: To the saints and faithful brethren in Christ which are at Colosse: Grace be unto you, and peace, from God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ.
kjv.COL_1_3: We give thanks to God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, praying always for you,
kjv.COL_1_4: Since we heard of your faith in Christ Jesus, and of the love which ye have to all the saints,
kjv.COL_1_5: For the hope which is laid up for you in heaven, whereof ye heard before in the word of the truth of the gospel;
kjv.COL_1_6: Which is come unto you, as it is in all the world; and bringeth forth fruit, as it doth also in you, since the day ye heard of it, and knew the grace of God in truth:
kjv.COL_1_7: As ye also learned of Epaphras our dear fellowservant, who is for you a faithful minister of Christ;
kjv.COL_1_8: Who also declared unto us your love in the Spirit.
kjv.COL_1_9: For this cause we also, since the day we heard it, do not cease to pray for you, and to desire that ye might be filled with the knowledge of his will in all wisdom and spiritual understanding;
kjv.COL_1_10: That ye might walk worthy of the Lord unto all pleasing, being fruitful in every good work, and increasing in the knowledge of God;
kjv.COL_1_11: Strengthened with all might, according to his glorious power, unto all patience and longsuffering with joyfulness;
kjv.COL_1_12: Giving thanks unto the Father, which hath made us meet to be partakers of the inheritance of the saints in light:
kjv.COL_1_13: Who hath delivered us from the power of darkness, and hath translated us into the kingdom of his dear Son:
kjv.COL_1_14: In whom we have redemption through his blood, even the forgiveness of sins:
kjv.COL_1_15: Who is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn of every creature:
kjv.COL_1_16: For by him were all things created, that are in heaven, and that are in earth, visible and invisible, whether they be thrones, or dominions, or principalities, or powers: all things were created by him, and for him:
kjv.COL_1_17: And he is before all things, and by him all things consist.
kjv.COL_1_18: And he is the head of the body, the church: who is the beginning, the firstborn from the dead; that in all things he might have the preeminence.
kjv.COL_1_19: For it pleased the Father that in him should all fulness dwell;
kjv.COL_1_20: And, having made peace through the blood of his cross, by him to reconcile all things unto himself; by him, I say, whether they be things in earth, or things in heaven.
kjv.COL_1_21: And you, that were sometime alienated and enemies in your mind by wicked works, yet now hath he reconciled
kjv.COL_1_22: In the body of his flesh through death, to present you holy and unblameable and unreproveable in his sight:
kjv.COL_1_23: If ye continue in the faith grounded and settled, and be not moved away from the hope of the gospel, which ye have heard, and which was preached to every creature which is under heaven; whereof I Paul am made a minister;
kjv.COL_1_24: Who now rejoice in my sufferings for you, and fill up that which is behind of the afflictions of Christ in my flesh for his body's sake, which is the church:
kjv.COL_1_25: Whereof I am made a minister, according to the dispensation of God which is given to me for you, to fulfil the word of God;
kjv.COL_1_26: Even the mystery which hath been hid from ages and from generations, but now is made manifest to his saints:
kjv.COL_1_27: To whom God would make known what is the riches of the glory of this mystery among the Gentiles; which is Christ in you, the hope of glory:
kjv.COL_1_28: Whom we preach, warning every man, and teaching every man in all wisdom; that we may present every man perfect in Christ Jesus:
kjv.COL_1_29: Whereunto I also labour, striving according to his working, which worketh in me mightily.
kjv.COL_2_1: For I would that ye knew what great conflict I have for you, and for them at Laodicea, and for as many as have not seen my face in the flesh;
kjv.COL_2_2: That their hearts might be comforted, being knit together in love, and unto all riches of the full assurance of understanding, to the acknowledgement of the mystery of God, and of the Father, and of Christ;
kjv.COL_2_3: In whom are hid all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
kjv.COL_2_4: And this I say, lest any man should beguile you with enticing words.
kjv.COL_2_5: For though I be absent in the flesh, yet am I with you in the spirit, joying and beholding your order, and the stedfastness of your faith in Christ.
kjv.COL_2_6: As ye have therefore received Christ Jesus the Lord, so walk ye in him:
kjv.COL_2_7: Rooted and built up in him, and stablished in the faith, as ye have been taught, abounding therein with thanksgiving.
kjv.COL_2_8: Beware lest any man spoil you through philosophy and vain deceit, after the tradition of men, after the rudiments of the world, and not after Christ.
kjv.COL_2_9: For in him dwelleth all the fulness of the Godhead bodily.
kjv.COL_2_10: And ye are complete in him, which is the head of all principality and power:
kjv.COL_2_11: In whom also ye are circumcised with the circumcision made without hands, in putting off the body of the sins of the flesh by the circumcision of Christ:
kjv.COL_2_12: Buried with him in baptism, wherein also ye are risen with him through the faith of the operation of God, who hath raised him from the dead.
kjv.COL_2_13: And you, being dead in your sins and the uncircumcision of your flesh, hath he quickened together with him, having forgiven you all trespasses;
kjv.COL_2_14: Blotting out the handwriting of ordinances that was against us, which was contrary to us, and took it out of the way, nailing it to his cross;
kjv.COL_2_15: And having spoiled principalities and powers, he made a shew of them openly, triumphing over them in it.
kjv.COL_2_16: Let no man therefore judge you in meat, or in drink, or in respect of an holyday, or of the new moon, or of the sabbath days:
kjv.COL_2_17: Which are a shadow of things to come; but the body is of Christ.
kjv.COL_2_18: Let no man beguile you of your reward in a voluntary humility and worshipping of angels, intruding into those things which he hath not seen, vainly puffed up by his fleshly mind,
kjv.COL_2_19: And not holding the Head, from which all the body by joints and bands having nourishment ministered, and knit together, increaseth with the increase of God.
kjv.COL_2_20: Wherefore if ye be dead with Christ from the rudiments of the world, why, as though living in the world, are ye subject to ordinances,
kjv.COL_2_22: Which all are to perish with the using;) after the commandments and doctrines of men?
kjv.COL_2_23: Which things have indeed a shew of wisdom in will worship, and humility, and neglecting of the body: not in any honour to the satisfying of the flesh.
kjv.COL_3_1: If ye then be risen with Christ, seek those things which are above, where Christ sitteth on the right hand of God.
kjv.COL_3_2: Set your affection on things above, not on things on the earth.
kjv.COL_3_3: For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.
kjv.COL_3_4: When Christ, who is our life, shall appear, then shall ye also appear with him in glory.
kjv.COL_3_5: Mortify therefore your members which are upon the earth; fornication, uncleanness, inordinate affection, evil concupiscence, and covetousness, which is idolatry:
kjv.COL_3_6: For which things' sake the wrath of God cometh on the children of disobedience:
kjv.COL_3_7: In the which ye also walked some time, when ye lived in them.
kjv.COL_3_8: But now ye also put off all these; anger, wrath, malice, blasphemy, filthy communication out of your mouth.
kjv.COL_3_9: Lie not one to another, seeing that ye have put off the old man with his deeds;
kjv.COL_3_10: And have put on the new man, which is renewed in knowledge after the image of him that created him:
kjv.COL_3_11: Where there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcision nor uncircumcision, Barbarian, Scythian, bond nor free: but Christ is all, and in all.
kjv.COL_3_12: Put on therefore, as the elect of God, holy and beloved, bowels of mercies, kindness, humbleness of mind, meekness, longsuffering;
kjv.COL_3_13: Forbearing one another, and forgiving one another, if any man have a quarrel against any: even as Christ forgave you, so also do ye.
kjv.COL_3_14: And above all these things put on charity, which is the bond of perfectness.
kjv.COL_3_15: And let the peace of God rule in your hearts, to the which also ye are called in one body; and be ye thankful.
kjv.COL_3_16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly in all wisdom; teaching and admonishing one another in psalms and hymns and spiritual songs, singing with grace in your hearts to the Lord.
kjv.COL_3_17: And whatsoever ye do in word or deed, do all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God and the Father by him.
kjv.COL_3_18: Wives, submit yourselves unto your own husbands, as it is fit in the Lord.
kjv.COL_3_19: Husbands, love your wives, and be not bitter against them.
kjv.COL_3_20: Children, obey your parents in all things: for this is well pleasing unto the Lord.
kjv.COL_3_21: Fathers, provoke not your children to anger, lest they be discouraged.
kjv.COL_3_22: Servants, obey in all things your masters according to the flesh; not with eyeservice, as menpleasers; but in singleness of heart, fearing God;
kjv.COL_3_23: And whatsoever ye do, do it heartily, as to the Lord, and not unto men;
kjv.COL_3_24: Knowing that of the Lord ye shall receive the reward of the inheritance: for ye serve the Lord Christ.
kjv.COL_3_25: But he that doeth wrong shall receive for the wrong which he hath done: and there is no respect of persons.
kjv.COL_4_1: Masters, give unto your servants that which is just and equal; knowing that ye also have a Master in heaven.
kjv.COL_4_2: Continue in prayer, and watch in the same with thanksgiving;
kjv.COL_4_3: Withal praying also for us, that God would open unto us a door of utterance, to speak the mystery of Christ, for which I am also in bonds:
kjv.COL_4_4: That I may make it manifest, as I ought to speak.
kjv.COL_4_5: Walk in wisdom toward them that are without, redeeming the time.
kjv.COL_4_6: Let your speech be always with grace, seasoned with salt, that ye may know how ye ought to answer every man.
kjv.COL_4_7: All my state shall Tychicus declare unto you, who is a beloved brother, and a faithful minister and fellowservant in the Lord:
kjv.COL_4_8: Whom I have sent unto you for the same purpose, that he might know your estate, and comfort your hearts;
kjv.COL_4_9: With Onesimus, a faithful and beloved brother, who is one of you. They shall make known unto you all things which are done here.
kjv.COL_4_10: Aristarchus my fellowprisoner saluteth you, and Marcus, sister's son to Barnabas, (touching whom ye received commandments: if he come unto you, receive him;)
kjv.COL_4_11: And Jesus, which is called Justus, who are of the circumcision. These only are my fellowworkers unto the kingdom of God, which have been a comfort unto me.
kjv.COL_4_12: Epaphras, who is one of you, a servant of Christ, saluteth you, always labouring fervently for you in prayers, that ye may stand perfect and complete in all the will of God.
kjv.COL_4_13: For I bear him record, that he hath a great zeal for you, and them that are in Laodicea, and them in Hierapolis.
kjv.COL_4_14: Luke, the beloved physician, and Demas, greet you.
kjv.COL_4_15: Salute the brethren which are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the church which is in his house.
kjv.COL_4_16: And when this epistle is read among you, cause that it be read also in the church of the Laodiceans; and that ye likewise read the epistle from Laodicea.
kjv.COL_4_17: And say to Archippus, Take heed to the ministry which thou hast received in the Lord, that thou fulfil it.
kjv.COL_4_18: The salutation by the hand of me Paul. Remember my bonds. Grace be with you. Amen.

net.COL_1_1: From Paul, an apostle of Christ Jesus by the will of God, and Timothy our brother,
net.COL_1_2: to the saints, the faithful brothers and sisters in Christ, at Colossae. Grace and peace to you from God our Father! Paul’s Thanksgiving and Prayer for the Church
net.COL_1_3: We always give thanks to God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you,
net.COL_1_4: since we heard about your faith in Christ Jesus and the love that you have for all the saints.
net.COL_1_5: Your faith and love have arisen from the hope laid up for you in heaven, which you have heard about in the message of truth, the gospel
net.COL_1_6: that has come to you. Just as in the entire world this gospel is bearing fruit and growing, so it has also been bearing fruit and growing among you from the first day you heard it and understood the grace of God in truth.
net.COL_1_7: You learned the gospel from Epaphras, our dear fellow slave —a faithful minister of Christ on our behalf—
net.COL_1_8: who also told us of your love in the Spirit. Paul’s Prayer for the Growth of the Church
net.COL_1_9: For this reason we also, from the day we heard about you, have not ceased praying for you and asking God to fill you with the knowledge of his will in all spiritual wisdom and understanding,
net.COL_1_10: so that you may live worthily of the Lord and please him in all respects —bearing fruit in every good deed, growing in the knowledge of God,
net.COL_1_11: being strengthened with all power according to his glorious might for the display of all patience and steadfastness, joyfully
net.COL_1_12: giving thanks to the Father who has qualified you to share in the saints’ inheritance in the light.
net.COL_1_13: He delivered us from the power of darkness and transferred us to the kingdom of the Son he loves,
net.COL_1_14: in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins. The Supremacy of Christ
net.COL_1_15: He is the image of the invisible God, the firstborn over all creation,
net.COL_1_16: for all things in heaven and on earth were created in him—all things, whether visible or invisible, whether thrones or dominions, whether principalities or powers—all things were created through him and for him.
net.COL_1_17: He himself is before all things and all things are held together in him.
net.COL_1_18: He is the head of the body, the church, as well as the beginning, the firstborn from the dead, so that he himself may become first in all things.
net.COL_1_19: For God was pleased to have all his fullness dwell in the Son
net.COL_1_20: and through him to reconcile all things to himself by making peace through the blood of his cross—through him, whether things on earth or things in heaven. Paul’s Goal in Ministry
net.COL_1_21: And you were at one time strangers and enemies in your minds as expressed through your evil deeds,
net.COL_1_22: but now he has reconciled you by his physical body through death to present you holy, without blemish, and blameless before him—
net.COL_1_23: if indeed you remain in the faith, established and firm, without shifting from the hope of the gospel that you heard. This gospel has also been preached in all creation under heaven, and I, Paul, have become its servant.
net.COL_1_24: Now I rejoice in my sufferings for you, and I fill up in my physical body—for the sake of his body, the church—what is lacking in the sufferings of Christ.
net.COL_1_25: I became a servant of the church according to the stewardship from God—given to me for you—in order to complete the word of God,
net.COL_1_26: that is, the mystery that has been kept hidden from ages and generations, but has now been revealed to his saints.
net.COL_1_27: God wanted to make known to them the glorious riches of this mystery among the Gentiles, which is Christ in you, the hope of glory.
net.COL_1_28: We proclaim him by instructing and teaching all people with all wisdom so that we may present every person mature in Christ.
net.COL_1_29: Toward this goal I also labor, struggling according to his power that powerfully works in me.
net.COL_2_1: For I want you to know how great a struggle I have for you, and for those in Laodicea, and for those who have not met me face to face.
net.COL_2_2: My goal is that their hearts, having been knit together in love, may be encouraged, and that they may have all the riches that assurance brings in their understanding of the knowledge of the mystery of God, namely, Christ,
net.COL_2_3: in whom are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
net.COL_2_4: I say this so that no one will deceive you through arguments that sound reasonable.
net.COL_2_5: For though I am absent from you in body, I am present with you in spirit, rejoicing to see your morale and the firmness of your faith in Christ. Warnings Against the Adoption of False Philosophies
net.COL_2_6: Therefore, just as you received Christ Jesus as Lord, continue to live your lives in him,
net.COL_2_7: rooted and built up in him and firm in your faith just as you were taught, and overflowing with thankfulness.
net.COL_2_8: Be careful not to allow anyone to captivate you through an empty, deceitful philosophy that is according to human traditions and the elemental spirits of the world, and not according to Christ.
net.COL_2_9: For in him all the fullness of deity lives in bodily form,
net.COL_2_10: and you have been filled in him, who is the head over every ruler and authority.
net.COL_2_11: In him you also were circumcised—not, however, with a circumcision performed by human hands, but by the removal of the fleshly body, that is, through the circumcision done by Christ.
net.COL_2_12: Having been buried with him in baptism, you also have been raised with him through your faith in the power of God who raised him from the dead.
net.COL_2_13: And even though you were dead in your transgressions and in the uncircumcision of your flesh, he nevertheless made you alive with him, having forgiven all your transgressions.
net.COL_2_14: He has destroyed what was against us, a certificate of indebtedness expressed in decrees opposed to us. He has taken it away by nailing it to the cross.
net.COL_2_15: Disarming the rulers and authorities, he has made a public disgrace of them, triumphing over them by the cross.
net.COL_2_16: Therefore do not let anyone judge you with respect to food or drink, or in the matter of a feast, new moon, or Sabbath days—
net.COL_2_17: these are only the shadow of the things to come, but the reality is Christ!
net.COL_2_18: Let no one who delights in false humility and the worship of angels pass judgment on you. That person goes on at great lengths about what he has supposedly seen, but he is puffed up with empty notions by his fleshly mind.
net.COL_2_19: He has not held fast to the head from whom the whole body, supported and knit together through its ligaments and sinews, grows with a growth that is from God.
net.COL_2_20: If you have died with Christ to the elemental spirits of the world, why do you submit to them as though you lived in the world?
net.COL_2_21: “Do not handle! Do not taste! Do not touch!”
net.COL_2_22: These are all destined to perish with use, founded as they are on human commands and teachings.
net.COL_2_23: Even though they have the appearance of wisdom with their self-imposed worship and humility achieved by an unsparing treatment of the body—a wisdom with no true value—they in reality result in fleshly indulgence.
net.COL_3_1: Therefore, if you have been raised with Christ, keep seeking the things above, where Christ is, seated at the right hand of God.
net.COL_3_2: Keep thinking about things above, not things on the earth,
net.COL_3_3: for you have died and your life is hidden with Christ in God.
net.COL_3_4: When Christ (who is your life) appears, then you too will be revealed in glory with him.
net.COL_3_5: So put to death whatever in your nature belongs to the earth: sexual immorality, impurity, shameful passion, evil desire, and greed which is idolatry.
net.COL_3_6: Because of these things the wrath of God is coming on the sons of disobedience.
net.COL_3_7: You also lived your lives in this way at one time, when you used to live among them.
net.COL_3_8: But now, put off all such things as anger, rage, malice, slander, abusive language from your mouth.
net.COL_3_9: Do not lie to one another since you have put off the old man with its practices
net.COL_3_10: and have been clothed with the new man that is being renewed in knowledge according to the image of the one who created it.
net.COL_3_11: Here there is neither Greek nor Jew, circumcised or uncircumcised, barbarian, Scythian, slave or free, but Christ is all and in all. Exhortation to Unity and Love
net.COL_3_12: Therefore, as the elect of God, holy and dearly loved, clothe yourselves with a heart of mercy, kindness, humility, gentleness, and patience,
net.COL_3_13: bearing with one another and forgiving one another, if someone happens to have a complaint against anyone else. Just as the Lord has forgiven you, so you also forgive others.
net.COL_3_14: And to all these virtues add love, which is the perfect bond.
net.COL_3_15: Let the peace of Christ be in control in your heart (for you were in fact called as one body to this peace), and be thankful.
net.COL_3_16: Let the word of Christ dwell in you richly, teaching and exhorting one another with all wisdom, singing psalms, hymns, and spiritual songs, all with grace in your hearts to God.
net.COL_3_17: And whatever you do in word or deed, do it all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.
net.COL_3_18: Wives, submit to your husbands, as is fitting in the Lord.
net.COL_3_19: Husbands, love your wives and do not be embittered against them.
net.COL_3_20: Children, obey your parents in everything, for this is pleasing in the Lord.
net.COL_3_21: Fathers, do not provoke your children, so they will not become disheartened.
net.COL_3_22: Slaves, obey your earthly masters in every respect, not only when they are watching—like those who are strictly people-pleasers—but with a sincere heart, fearing the Lord.
net.COL_3_23: Whatever you are doing, work at it with enthusiasm, as to the Lord and not for people,
net.COL_3_24: because you know that you will receive your inheritance from the Lord as the reward. Serve the Lord Christ.
net.COL_3_25: For the one who does wrong will be repaid for his wrong, and there are no exceptions.
net.COL_4_1: Masters, treat your slaves with justice and fairness, because you know that you also have a master in heaven. Exhortation to Pray for the Success of Paul’s Mission
net.COL_4_2: Be devoted to prayer, keeping alert in it with thanksgiving.
net.COL_4_3: At the same time pray for us too, that God may open a door for the message so that we may proclaim the mystery of Christ, for which I am in chains.
net.COL_4_4: Pray that I may make it known as I should.
net.COL_4_5: Conduct yourselves with wisdom toward outsiders, making the most of the opportunities.
net.COL_4_6: Let your speech always be gracious, seasoned with salt, so that you may know how you should answer everyone. Personal Greetings and Instructions
net.COL_4_7: Tychicus, a dear brother, faithful minister, and fellow slave in the Lord, will tell you all the news about me.
net.COL_4_8: I sent him to you for this very purpose, that you may know how we are doing and that he may encourage your hearts.
net.COL_4_9: I sent him with Onesimus, the faithful and dear brother, who is one of you. They will tell you about everything here.
net.COL_4_10: Aristarchus, my fellow prisoner, sends you greetings, as does Mark, the cousin of Barnabas (about whom you received instructions; if he comes to you, welcome him).
net.COL_4_11: And Jesus who is called Justus also sends greetings. In terms of Jewish converts, these are the only fellow workers for the kingdom of God, and they have been a comfort to me.
net.COL_4_12: Epaphras, who is one of you and a slave of Christ, greets you. He is always struggling in prayer on your behalf, so that you may stand mature and fully assured in all the will of God.
net.COL_4_13: For I can testify that he has worked hard for you and for those in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
net.COL_4_14: Our dear friend Luke the physician and Demas greet you.
net.COL_4_15: Give my greetings to the brothers and sisters who are in Laodicea and to Nympha and the church that meets in her house.
net.COL_4_16: And after you have read this letter, have it read to the church of Laodicea. In turn, read the letter from Laodicea as well.
net.COL_4_17: And tell Archippus, “See to it that you complete the ministry you received in the Lord.”
net.COL_4_18: I, Paul, write this greeting by my own hand. Remember my chains. Grace be with you.
